mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production below negative 1.0 percent reflects immediate manufacturing weakness, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of observations following an INDPRO decline exceeding 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate indicate a successful soft-landing policy stance, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 4.0 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production capacity supports higher corporate output and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rate exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant relationship with 60-day forward equity returns over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
